NYC Community Well-Being and Public Safety

Incarceration Rate i
Number of jail admissions per 10,000 residents in the neighborhood between 2023 and 2025. Higher means more frequent contact with the jail system per resident.

High

Well-Being Category i
Neighborhoods are grouped based on their overall Well-Being Score compared to the NYC average. Higher scores indicate stronger overall community well-being.
– – Higher well-being than the NYC average.
– – Similar well-being to the NYC average.
– – Lower well-being than the NYC average.
Parks, airports, and similar areas excluded from the analysis.
Above Average WB
Near the Average WB
Below Average WB

Incarceration i 2 indicators
▾
Incarceration

Incarceration Count - The number of jail admissions of residents in a given neighborhood between 2023 and 2025.

Incarceration Rate - Number of jail admissions per 10,000 residents in the neighborhood between 2023 and 2025. Higher means more frequent contact with the jail system per resident.

Health i 1 indicator
▾
Health Index
Life expectancy at birth for residents in a given neighborhood. Higher means people live longer, healthier lives. Source: NYC DOHMH, 2011-2020.
Hover Over or click an NTA to see values

Education i 1 indicator
▾
Education Index
A measure of school enrollment and degree attainment among residents. Higher means more residents are in school or have completed degrees. Source: Measure of America, DATA2GO.NYC, 2022.
Hover or click an NTA to see values

Economic Security i 1 indicator
▾
Poverty %
Share of residents living in households with income below the federal poverty line. Higher means more residents are in economic hardship. Source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS, 2020-2024.

Housing i 1 indicator
▾
Housing & Building Rate
311 complaints about housing conditions (e.g., heating, hot water, plumbing, paint, electric, structure) per 10,000 residents. Higher means more reported problems with where people live. Source: NYC 311, 2025.
Hover or click an NTA to see values
Housing & Building Rate --
Avg -
vs Avg -
Civic Infrastructure i 1 indicator
▾
Sanitation & Noise Rate
311 complaints about sanitation issues (e.g., dirty conditions, illegal dumping, rodents) and noise (e.g., residential, commercial, street) per 10,000 residents. Higher means more reported environmental and quality-of-life issues. Source: NYC 311, 2025.
